Anthracnose.overview: Caused by Colletotrichum gloeosporioides. Appears as dark, sunken lesions on leaves.
treatment: Apply copper-based fungicides (Copper Oxychloride 50% WP) at 2–3 g/L.
Spray Mancozeb every 10–14 days in wet seasons.
prevention: Maintain proper tree spacing for air flow.

Colletorichum-leaf-disease.overview: Caused by Colletotrichum gloeosporioides. Appears as dark, sunken lesions on leaves.
treatment: Apply copper-based fungicides (Copper Oxychloride 50% WP) at 2–3 g/L.
Spray Mancozeb every 10–14 days in wet seasons.
prevention: Maintain proper tree spacing for air flow.

Birds-eye.overview: Caused by Helminthosporium heveae. Named for the bird's-eye pattern on infected leaves.
treatment: Use Chlorothalonil 75% WP as a contact fungicide.

Corynespora.overview: Caused by Corynespora cassiicola. One of the most serious rubber leaf diseases.
treatment: Apply Hexaconazole 5% EC at 2 mL/L (Highly effective).
Remove heavily infected trees in outbreak zones.

Healthy.overview: The leaf shows no signs of disease.

Dry_Leaf.overview: Physiological or stress-related drying, often associated with drought or nutrient deficiency.
treatment: Apply foliar spray of Urea + ZnSO4.

Leaf_Spot.overview: General category of fungal leaf spot diseases.
prevention: Reduce leaf wetness by improving canopy air flow.

Pesta.overview: Powdery scab-like disease affecting leaves in humid conditions.

Powdery-mildew.overview: Caused by Oidium heveae. Appears as powdery white spots on young leaves.
